meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier. Learn to build professional apps at speed using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led freelancer tutorial.
content.answer_block: Stop building from scratch. Use a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to bypass the complexity of authentication, database schemas, and billing integrations. By combining Antigravity’s architecture generation with Claude Code’s autonomous engineering, you can ship production-ready SaaS products in hours rather than weeks, effectively neutralizing your lack of deep SaaS knowledge.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambitious ideas. You have the client, you have the vision, but the technical abyss of authentication, Stripe webhooks, and database migrations stands between you and your paycheck. It is the classic 'no SaaS knowledge' trap—where high-potential freelancers lose weeks to boilerplate code instead of delivering value.

But the game has changed. We are no longer writing every line of code; we are orchestrating high-performance systems. By leveraging an opinionated SaaS starter kit, you transform from a manual coder into a technical architect. This is your blueprint to shipping professional-grade SaaS applications while others are still setting up their environment variables.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Architecture
steps.description: Initialize your project structure using Antigravity to generate a secure, scalable foundation. This ensures your directory structure follows industry best practices immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust SaaS foundation using Next.js 14, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include user authentication, protected routes, and a clean dashboard layout.

steps.title: Automate Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models without writing SQL. Let Claude Code handle the migration files based on your product requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for a subscription-based SaaS. Include tables for profiles, subscriptions, and usage logs with row-level security policies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply prisma schema changes and run migrations'

steps.title: Integrate Payment Infrastructure
steps.description: Stripe integration is the hardest part of SaaS. Use the starter kit's pre-built webhooks to handle subscription lifecycles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure Stripe webhooks to handle subscription creation, cancellation, and invoice payment success events in a Next.js API route.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'connect stripe-webhook handler to the user subscription status update'

steps.title: Implement Role-Based Access
steps.description: Secure your application by defining distinct user roles. This prevents unauthorized access to admin-only dashboard features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement RBAC middleware that restricts dashboard access to authenticated users with a 'pro' subscription level.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add auth middleware to protect /dashboard routes'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Finalize the environment configuration and push your build to a global CDN for lightning-fast performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production deployment checklist for Vercel, including environment variable management and build optimization settings.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify production build readiness and optimize for speed'

pro_tips: Always separate your business logic from UI components to make future pivots painless.
Use mock data early in development to test UI states before the backend is fully wired.
Keep your Claude Code context window lean by deleting unnecessary log files before long sessions.
Automate your CI/CD pipeline from day one; if it isn't deployed, it doesn't exist for the client.
Prioritize 'feature flags' over hard-coding; it allows you to toggle features for beta testers without redeploying.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, audit your workflow to identify which components can be turned into your own custom starter template for the next client.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting technical debt dictate your growth. With these tools, you have the power to build like a senior engineer and charge accordingly. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
